summary: Level up Lady is a women's personal-development brand run by certified life coach Brittany Cherrell, offering a 100+ article blog, free self-improvement eBooks and workbooks, and (implied) low-cost courses and 1:1 coaching, hosted on a Teachable school. It serves budget-conscious women of any age who feel stuck or under-confident and want practical inner-work tools without paying for expensive coaching.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new eBook downloader to her first mindset win and set expectations for what Level up Lady is.
campaigns.trigger: Free '30 Days of Quotes' eBook + workbook signup on Teachable (list join).
campaigns.segment: All new contacts; no survey data yet, so lead with the universal 'stuck / hard to get up in the morning' pain.
campaigns.rationale: Right now new downloaders get only Teachable's default receipt and then silence — this is the single biggest leak. The welcome email is the most-opened send a brand makes and converts a one-time download into an ongoing relationship.

emails.purpose: Warm sisterly welcome, deliver the eBook link, and walk her through Day 1 so she gets an immediate mindset win.
emails.subject: Hey Lady — your 30-Day workbook is inside (start with Day 1)
emails.preview: Two minutes today and you'll feel the shift. I'll show you exactly where to begin.
keyPoints: Open warm and personal in Brittany's voice ('Hey Lady!') — name the shared feeling of waking up to a life that's hard to get up for
Give the direct download/access link to the 30 Days of Quotes eBook + workbook so there's zero friction
Point to ONE action: read today's quote and answer the Day 1 workbook prompt — frame it as a 2-minute first win
Set expectations: a few short emails coming with free tools and blog reads, no fluff, no big price tags
Invite a real reply to brittany@leveluplady.co — a real person reads every one

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Keep new downloaders engaged past Day 1, build trust with free blog value, and warm them toward working more closely with Brittany.
campaigns.trigger: Completion of (or 3+ days into) the 30-day eBook; runs after the welcome sequence.
campaigns.segment: eBook downloaders below any 'ready to invest' signal; branch later by which blog topics they click (confidence, transitions, motivation).
campaigns.rationale: The eBook is the whole top of funnel and 100+ blog articles sit unused in email — nurture turns a one-time freebie into a habit and is the bridge to the brand's first paid conversions. A buyer-journey-mapped nurture has beaten plain newsletters by +650% CTR.

emails.purpose: Education email that teaches one repeatable inner-work framework and reframes the 'I'm stuck' problem, pulling in a relevant free blog article.
emails.subject: The real reason mornings feel so heavy (it's fixable)
emails.preview: Confidence isn't something you're born with — here's the small daily practice that builds it.
emails.cta: Read the full breakdown on the blog
keyPoints: Name the pain in her words — a life that's hard to get up to — then reframe it as a fixable mindset pattern, not a personality flaw
Teach one concrete, free practice she can do today (e.g. a confidence or reframe ritual) drawn from the 30-day work
Link to one resonant blog article that goes deeper, building the habit of coming back to Level up Lady
Keep it budget-conscious and non-pushy — value first, no ask beyond reading
Soft thread toward 'there's more if you want to go deeper' to set up the conversion campaign

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Conversion
campaigns.objective: Turn an engaged free reader into a paying client by inviting her to a low-friction next step with Brittany.
campaigns.trigger: Engagement milestone — finished the eBook and/or clicked multiple blog/nurture links (sales-ready signal).
campaigns.segment: Warmed nurture contacts showing repeated engagement; address the explicit #1 objection: 'I can't afford coaching.'
campaigns.rationale: There's currently no conversion path at all — coaching is inquiry-only with no visible CTA, so engaged readers have nowhere to go. A soft, affordability-forward invite to a free discovery chat (or low-ticket course) creates the brand's first real revenue motion.

emails.purpose: Invite the warmed reader to a free, no-pressure discovery chat (or next low-cost offer), directly disarming the price objection.
emails.subject: Want to do this together? (no big price tag, promise)
emails.preview: You've done the inner work solo — here's a free, no-pressure chat about what's next for you.
emails.cta: Reply or book a free chat with Brittany
keyPoints: Acknowledge how far she's already come on her own through the free content — credit her effort
Tackle the affordability objection head-on: working with Brittany is built to be budget-conscious, this first chat is free
Make the next step tiny — reply to this email or grab a free discovery call to talk through where she feels stuck
Use proof of transformation (Brittany's own story / a peer's reframe) to handle the 'will this work for me' doubt
One clear CTA only; warm, sisterly, zero hard-sell pressure

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and keep the list warm between launches by delivering one useful inner-work idea on a steady cadence.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled cadence (weekly or biweekly); suppress contacts still in the welcome sequence and route dormant ones to win-back.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts who've finished onboarding; later segment editions by interest tag (confidence, transitions, entrepreneurship).
campaigns.rationale: The email list is the brand's primary retention lever and it's underused, while 100+ blog articles give endless newsletter fuel. A recurring 'Lady, this week' habit fights the brand's high passive churn without ever needing a discount.

emails.purpose: Establish the repeatable newsletter template with one actionable tip and a single primary CTA to read more.
emails.subject: This week, Lady: the 5-minute confidence reset
emails.preview: One small practice to start your week feeling a little more like yourself.
keyPoints: Open with a recurring, habit-building section ('This week, Lady…') so she learns to expect and open it
Deliver ONE useful idea — a quick tip, mini-reframe, or behind-the-scenes from Brittany's own week
80% value / 20% soft offer — point to a blog article or free resource, not a hard sell
Single primary CTA (read / reply), keep it short and skimmable
Sisterly, encouraging tone with an open invitation to reply and share what she's working on

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant downloaders who went quiet and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold — 60–90 days with no opens or clicks.
campaigns.segment: Dormant free-content subscribers who never converted; lean on fresh value and a reminder of why she signed up.
campaigns.rationale: Free-content consumers have zero financial commitment and disengage silently — this is the brand's stated #1 churn risk. A short win-back recovers 5–15% of them and sunsets the rest so the small list stays deliverable.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with a warm 'we miss you' that reminds her of the outcome she came for.
emails.subject: Did we lose you, Lady?
emails.preview: You came here to feel more like yourself again. That offer still stands.
emails.cta: Come back & pick one thing to read
keyPoints: Lead with warmth, not guilt — remind her of the change she wanted when she first downloaded the eBook
Offer one fresh, high-value reason to return (a new or best-of blog article / refreshed free resource)
Make returning effortless — a single low-commitment click to one resource
Reaffirm the budget-conscious promise: it's all still free to come back to
Set up the rest of the sequence to escalate to a soft sunset ('should we stop emailing you?') for non-responders
